provenance.rationale: Round_01 hit primary-metric saturation: mean_hit_at_5 = 0.76 across all 12 COMPLETE trials (statistics.std_value = 1.1e-16). With zero objective variance, fANOVA importance defaulted to uniform 0.125 across every param (statistics shows no signal). The best-trial bundle's secondary section reveals that mean_mrr_at_10 (0.664) and mean_ndcg_at_10 (0.6882) DO vary across the trial set, and the wide-MMR-titlecap diagnostic at retrieval-wide-mmr-titlecap-20260429-1353 confirms MRR moved 0.6587→0.6699 across MMR variants on the full 200-row dataset. This round therefore swaps the objective to mean_mrr_at_10 — rank-sensitive and observed to vary on the same offline subset — without touching the search space. Search space stays identical because: (a) statistics.axis_coverage[mmr_lambda] flags both the 0.55 and 0.75 edges as UNSAMPLED in round_01's COMPLETE trials, so anti-pattern A10 forbids narrowing mmr_lambda; (b) every categorical was sampled at least once but with zero objective variance there is no evidence to drop any choice (anti-pattern A12). n_trials raised 12→16 to give TPE more data on the new metric; n_startup_trials kept at 4 since the search space is unchanged. Sampler seed bumped 42→43 so re-running round_02 doesn't replay round_01's exact param sequence, which is necessary because the metric change makes those trial values not directly comparable.

diff_summary.evidence: statistics.std_value = 1.1e-16 over 12 complete trials; best_trial.user_attrs.secondary.mean_mrr_at_10 = 0.664 vs configs in trials 5/8/9 with secondary MRR ~0.65-0.67 → measurable variance
param_importances all 0.125 in round_01 → TPE never escaped startup phase; bumping trials gives TPE 12 post-startup trials on the new metric
reseed avoids replaying round_01's exact param sequence which is now invalid for the new objective

notes: Search space unchanged from round_01. mmr_lambda edges UNSAMPLED — A10 forbids narrowing. Categoricals all sampled but zero objective variance → A12 forbids dropping choices. The metric switch is the only optimization-affecting change.
